channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran's Key Demands for Ending US Conflict Emerge Amid Indirect Talks and Trump's Claims
item.description: As the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran continues, fresh signals from Tehran indicate that any negotiations for truce will come with strict and possibly non-negotiable conditions. U...

item.title: Five legal professionals shot in the past 10 months, highlighting safety concerns in the legal field
item.description: In the past 10 months, five legal professionals have been shot, with four of them fatally. The most recent case was that of Chinette Gallichan, a labour lawyer who was killed outside the Commission fo...

item.title: A Chicago Family’s Experience Living in Fear of ICE Raids
item.description: Ava and Sam, an undocumented family living in Chicago, have spent months in hiding due to the threat of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) raids. Their fear began in the fall when ICE patrols b...

item.title: Transnet faces parliamentary scrutiny on rising debt, governance issues and audit outcomes
item.description: Transnet, South Africa's state-owned logistics company, came under sharp questioning in Parliament during a briefing to the Standing Committee on Public Accounts (Scopa) regarding its 2024/25 audit ou...

item.title: Kenya’s Climate Crisis: Adaptation and the Role of Humanitarian Aid
item.description: Kenya is currently facing a double climate crisis, with devastating floods in some areas and severe droughts in others. This has led to widespread economic losses, displaced families, and severe food ...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh Minister urges officials to expedite irrigation projects to meet deadlines
item.description: In a recent video conference reviewing major irrigation projects, Andhra Pradesh Water Resources Minister Nimmala Ramanaidu expressed serious concern over delays, particularly in the tunnel lining wor...

item.title: Alex Eala Shares Reflections on Her Breakthrough Week at the Miami Open and Representing the Philippines
item.description: Filipino tennis star Alex Eala described her recent experience at the Miami Open as probably the craziest week of her life. Entering the tournament ranked around 140th in the world as a wild card, the...

item.title: FG Apologises Over Power Shortages, Pledges Measures to Boost Supply
item.description: The Federal Government don apologise to Nigerians over di wahala wey dey happen for power supply, as Minister of Power, Adebayo Adelabu, talk say dem dey work on improvements wey go soon reach every p...

item.title: Pak-Afghan clerics urge extension of ceasefire till Eidul Azha
item.description: On March 25, 2026, clerics from Pakistan and Afghanistan issued a joint appeal to extend the temporary pause in fighting between the two nations, which had initially been declared for Eidul Fitr. Paki...

item.title: Understanding Air Hunger: Causes, Symptoms and Management
item.description: Air hunger is the intense, urgent sensation that you can’t get enough air, which differs from general shortness of breath. People might experience it during strenuous exercise, but for those with lung...

item.title: Mohamed Salah to depart Liverpool after remarkable nine-year tenure
item.description: Mohamed Salah, the Egyptian forward for Liverpool, has announced that he will be leaving the club at the conclusion of the 2025/26 season. At 33 years old, Salah has enjoyed an exceptional nine-year c...

item.title: Israel plans to occupy southern Lebanon in bid for defensive buffer as Hezbollah vows resistance
item.description: Israel's Defence Minister, Israel Katz, has announced plans to seize parts of southern Lebanon up to the Litani River, claiming it will create a 'defensive buffer' to protect Israel. This move would m...

item.title: PM Modi holds phone talks with Sri Lanka President on global energy crisis triggered by US-Iran conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi spoke on phone with Sri Lankan President Anura Kumara Dissanayake on Tuesday, focusing mainly on the serious global energy crisis caused by the ongoing US-Iran war in West...

item.title: Tezgam Express derails near Lodhran, multiple passengers injured
item.description: LAHORE: On Tuesday night, the Karachi-bound Tezgam Express met with a serious accident near Lodhran in Punjab, causing injuries to several passengers. According to the Lodhran District Emergency Offic...

item.title: Premiers Call for Increased Provincial Input on Judicial Appointments
item.description: The premiers of Ontario, Quebec, Alberta, and Saskatchewan have sent a letter to Prime Minister Mark Carney requesting a larger role in the selection of judges for superior trial courts and courts of ...

item.title: Personal Reflections on Pluralism and Tolerance from Sri Lanka’s Civil War
item.description: In this reflective piece, a personality psychologist who grew up in Sri Lanka shares how his experiences during the country’s civil war (1983–2009) shaped his understanding of pluralism. Raised in the...

item.title: Australian shares set to rise as Ares restricts private credit fund withdrawals
item.description: Australian shares are expected to open higher, with ASX 200 futures up 48 points to 8465, trimming earlier gains of more than 75 points. US equities showed mixed performance, with the S&P 500 down 0.2...

item.title: Philippines Declares National Energy Emergency Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. declared a national energy emergency on March 24, 2026, due to disruptions caused by the ongoing war between the US, Israel, and Iran. The conflict has signif...

item.title: Kibra Court to Decide on Raphael Tuju's Bail and Case Jurisdiction
item.description: Nairobi – The Kibra Court is set to make a ruling at noon on Wednesday regarding former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju’s challenge to the DCI case against him and his application for bail pending arra...

item.title: Power Ministry outlines plans to restore electricity supply within two weeks
item.description: Adebayo Adelabu, di Minister of Power, don yan say government dey put measures to fix di wahala wey dey gas supply wey dey affect power supply for Nigeria. For press conference wey happen for Abuja on...
